nodes.name: Email source
nodes.notes: Passthrough node that unifies the two intake paths (Sample email for manual test, Normalize Gmail for real inbox). Downstream nodes always reference $('Email source').

nodes.name: Sample email
nodes.notes: Stand-in for the Gmail / IMAP trigger. Swap this node for an Email Trigger (IMAP) node once credentials are configured — the rest of the workflow consumes the same {senderEmail, subject, body, senderName} fields.

assignments.name: subject
assignments.value: Ne mogu se prijaviti na svoj račun već 3 dana

assignments.name: body
assignments.value:
Poštovani,

već treći dan pokušavam ući u svoj račun i jednostavno ne ide. Kad upišem email i lozinku, samo mi izbacuje poruku "Pogrešni podaci za prijavu" iako sam 100% sigurna da unosim ispravnu lozinku — koristim istu već godinama.

Probala sam i opciju "Zaboravljena lozinka" — kliknem na link, upišem email, dobijem poruku da je email poslan, ali ništa ne stiže. Ni u inboxu ni u spamu. Čekala sam pola sata, pa probala opet — isto. Provjerila sam pišem li ispravan email, pišem.

Ne znam što više. Imam narudžbu koju sam htjela pratiti i sad ne mogu uopće pristupiti računu. Možete li mi pomoći resetirati račun ili mi reći što se događa?

Hvala unaprijed,
Marina

parameters.subject: =Re: {{ $('Merge verdict + context').first().json.email.subject }}
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Merge verdict + context').first().json.customer.firstName || ($('Merge verdict + context').first().json.email.senderName || 'there').split(' ')[0] }},

Thank you for reaching out to our Customer Support team.

{{ $('Merge verdict + context').first().json.response }}

If this resolves your question, no further action is needed. If you need additional help, simply reply to this email and we will get back to you.

Best regards,
Customer Support Team

options.systemMessage:
=You are a customer-support classifier and responder for an e-commerce store.

AVAILABLE CATEGORIES (from the case_categories table — single source of truth):
{{ $('Fetch categories').first().json.map(c => c.name).join(' | ') }}

PROCESS
1. Read the customer email (subject + body) and pick the most likely category
   from the list above. If the email genuinely fits none of them, use "Other".

2. Call `search_knowledge_base` once, passing that category. The tool returns up
   to 3 FAQ articles plus a relevance score from a Cohere cross-encoder reranker.
   Higher score = the article answers the customer's specific scenario more directly.

3. Read the returned articles AND their relevance scores before drafting anything.
   Treat the scores as evidence for how confident you should be:
     score >= 0.7  : article directly addresses the question — quote/paraphrase it
     score 0.4-0.7 : article frames the topic but doesn't answer the exact subcase
     score < 0.4   : article is only loosely related — do NOT lean on it

4. If the best-scoring article still doesn't cover the question, you MAY call the
   tool one more time with a DIFFERENT category guess (any other name from the list).
   Do not call more than twice.

5. Use ONLY the retrieved articles to draft the response. NEVER invent policies,
   timelines, or contact channels that aren't in the retrieved text.

CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION
The `confidence` field is a HOLISTIC judgement combining:
  (a) classification certainty (is the category obvious?), AND
  (b) retrieval groundedness (does an article directly answer the question?)

Use these tiers — do not exceed the cap implied by retrieval quality:
  0.90 - 1.00 : Category is obvious AND a top-scoring article (>= 0.7) directly
                addresses the customer's exact scenario.
  0.70 - 0.89 : Category is obvious AND an article (0.4-0.7) covers the policy frame
                but you had to extrapolate one step to answer the specific case.
  0.50 - 0.69 : Category obvious but no article scored above 0.4. You cite the
                general policy but the specific detail isn't in the knowledge base.
  < 0.50      : The retrieved articles do NOT adequately cover the question.
                The response MUST be a polite acknowledgement that the case will
                be routed to a human specialist. Do NOT fabricate procedures.

OUTPUT (return STRICT JSON matching the attached schema)
- category   : one of the names from the AVAILABLE CATEGORIES list above.
- categoryId : the integer categoryId of that category (look it up in
               {{ JSON.stringify($('Fetch categories').first().json.map(c => ({id: c.categoryId, name: c.name}))) }})
- priority   : CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW
- confidence : 0..1, per the calibration tiers above
- summary    : one sentence describing the customer's issue
- response   : the BODY of the reply only — NO greeting ("Hi X,"), NO sign-off
               ("Best regards," etc). Those are added automatically by the email
               template. Up to 5 sentences, polite and direct, grounded ONLY in
               the retrieved articles. If confidence < 0.5, write a one-paragraph
               acknowledgement that the case is being routed to a human
               specialist who will follow up shortly.

parameters.toolDescription: Search the customer-support FAQ knowledge base. Pass the email's category (Billing | Technical | Account | Product) to filter retrieval to only that domain. Returns the top 3 most relevant articles after cross-encoder reranking.

metadataValues.name: category
metadataValues.value: ={{ $fromAI('category', 'The email category — exactly one of: Billing, Technical, Account, Product', 'string') }}

parameters.subject: =[#{{ $('Create case (open)').first().json.caseNumber }}] We received your message
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Merge verdict + context').first().json.customer.firstName || ($('Merge verdict + context').first().json.email.senderName || 'there').split(' ')[0] }},

Thanks for reaching out — we've received your inquiry and opened case #{{ $('Create case (open)').first().json.caseNumber }} for tracking.

A support agent will review your message and get back to you as soon as possible. There's no need to reply to this acknowledgement — we'll be in touch directly from your case.

If you have additional details to share in the meantime, simply reply to this email and we'll attach them to the case.

Best regards,
Customer Support Team
